summary: Ukraine's SBU struck Russian military support vessels 'Volga' and 'Vyatka' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, targeting submarine reconnaissance and mine-laying systems along with S-400 components covering the Kerch Strait area. A massive Russian drone and missile campaign targeted energy infrastructure across Poltava, Kremenchuk, and Sumy regions, with multiple Shahed strikes on the Kremenchuk HES, TES, and oil refinery. Russia and Ukraine exchanged 160 prisoners of war each, while President Zelensky signed a decree mandating at least 26% of GDP for security and defense in the 2027 budget.
key_points: SBU struck Russian military vessels 'Volga,' 'Vyatka,' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, along with S-400 radar and weapons components covering the Kerch Strait. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

Russian forces launched massive drone attacks on energy infrastructure in Poltava region and targeted Kremenchuk HES, TES, and oil refinery with multiple Shahed drones. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68071, @mon1tor_ua/68069, @mon1tor_ua/68062)
Russia and Ukraine exchanged 160 prisoners of war each, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187, @tass_agency/382553)

President Zelensky signed decree No. 528/2026 mandating at least 26% of GDP for security and defense in the 2027 budget, with control assigned to NSDC Secretary Rustem Umerov. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23597)

Russian ballistic missile struck Zaporizhzhia, injuring at least six people, and Russian forces attacked TCC and weapons storage facilities in Kyiv with group strikes. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190, @tass_agency/382553)
NATO Secretary General Rutte stated Ukraine is performing well, particularly in drone technology, and that Russian oil refinery production has decreased by one-third. (Source: @UkraineNow/82188)

At the Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdansk, 160 agreements worth over €10 billion were signed, including a first EU tranche of €3.2 billion and $3.4 billion from the World Bank. (Source: @UkraineNow/82185)
Hungary refused to sign an EU proposal to open negotiations with Ukraine on additional issues, with Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar calling it illogical. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31172)

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine's SBU struck Russian military support vessels 'Volga' and 'Vyatka' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, targeting submarine reconnaissance systems, mine-laying capabilities, and S-400 components covering the Kerch Strait. Russia launched a massive coordinated drone and missile campaign targeting energy infrastructure across Poltava, Kremenchuk, and Sumy regions, with multiple Shahed strikes on the Kremenchuk HES, TES, and oil refinery within hours. The conflict continues to escalate with both sides conducting strategic strikes on critical infrastructure while engaging in a 160-for-160 prisoner exchange.

points: The SBU struck Russian military support vessels 'Volga' and 'Vyatka' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea; these vessels were built for the Russian Defense Ministry to deploy submarine reconnaissance observation systems and lay mines targeting ships, pipelines, and cables. Additionally, Ukrainian forces struck weapons and radar components from an S-400 complex covering the Kerch Strait area. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

President Zelensky signed decree No. 528/2026 implementing an NSDC decision from May 19, mandating the Cabinet include at least 26% of GDP for security and defense in the 2027 state budget, with this priority to remain through 2028-2029. Control over execution was assigned to NSDC Secretary Rustem Umerov. For comparison, the 2026 budget allocated 27.2% of GDP (2.806 trillion UAH) and 2025 allocated 26.3% of GDP. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23597)

At the Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdansk, 160 agreements worth over €10 billion were signed, including a first EU tranche of €3.2 billion, $3.4 billion under a World Bank agreement, launch of the European Flagship Fund for Ukraine Reconstruction, creation of the Ukraine Transport Support Fund, and €140 million for housing programs. (Source: @UkraineNow/82185)

Ukraine conducted a 160-for-160 prisoner of war exchange with Russia, as confirmed by both the Russian Defense Ministry and statements from Ukrainian officials including Budanov, who indicated the exchange could take place in the coming days. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187, @UkraineNow/82192)

Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga stated that Belarus is not expected to enter the war, saying 'we assume that they will not allow Russia to drag them into this war, because Ukraine will mirror it.' (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191)

GUR MO conducted an urgent fundraising campaign for long-range FPV drones on fiber optics capable of destroying Russian equipment heading to Crimea or the front line, with the stated goal of destroying Russian equipment before it reaches the front line. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68080)

In Kharkov, a man attacked TCC employees with a knife, killing one military commissar and wounding another; the attacker is currently wanted. In Odessa, a man in flip-flops broke a car window trying to intervene when TCC employees took a dog. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31180, @ukr_leaks_eng/31173)

Russian forces launched massive drone attacks on energy infrastructure in Poltava region and deployed multiple Shahed drones targeting Kremenchuk HES (at least 3 separate strikes reported), Kremenchuk TES, and Kremenchuk oil refinery within a concentrated timeframe. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68071, @mon1tor_ua/68069, @mon1tor_ua/68068, @mon1tor_ua/68062, @mon1tor_ua/68072)

A ballistic missile was launched toward Kyiv, and Russian forces conducted group strikes on TCC (military commissariat) facilities and weapons storage facilities in the capital. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68045, @tass_agency/382553)

Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia with ballistic weapons, injuring at least six people. The enemy also struck the building of the design engineering department of the Zaporizhzhia NPP in the industrial zone. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190, @ukr_leaks_eng/31178)

Russian forces deployed Lancet-type ('Geranium-4/5') reactive drones launched from Orel toward multiple directions including Kharkiv, Balakliya, Vovchany, Izyum, Putyvl, Okhtyrka, and Kursk-area targets; drones were tracked across Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Poltava regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66469, @kpszsu/66499, @kpszsu/66473, @kpszsu/66496, @kpszsu/66480, @eRadarrua/98257, @mon1tor_ua/68044)
In Crimea and Sevastopol, regional emergency regimes were introduced for economic decision-making, according to Aksenov. (Source: @tass_agency/382552, @tass_agency/382553)
Returned Russian POWs from Ukrainian captivity are in Belarus, with ombudsman Lantratova working with them, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382523)

Former Russian Defense Minister Sergei Ivanov, honorary president of the United VTB League, died at age 73. The Russian Justice Ministry expanded its foreign agents list to include 'Crimean Solidarity,' 'Echo' project, sociologist Elena Koneva, and 'Autonomous Action' media group. (Source: @tass_agency/382524, @tass_agency/382559)

Russian drones were tracked across northern and eastern Kharkiv region heading toward Bohodukhiv, Balakliya, Vovchany, and Izyum, with reactive-type drones also moving from Donetsk region toward southern Kharkiv region in the direction of Lozova. (Source: @kpszsu/66499, @kpszsu/66496, @kpszsu/66473)

Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia with ballistic weapons, injuring at least six people, and hit the design engineering department building of the Zaporizhzhia NPP in the industrial zone. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190, @ukr_leaks_eng/31178)

In Zaporizhzhia region, a Ukrainian UAV struck a private household causing a residential house fire; a drone attacked a food delivery vehicle in Kamenka-Dneprovskaya damaging a store and vehicle; two men were wounded in Vasylovka district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178)
Traffic on the R-280 'Novorossiya' highway from kilometer 317 to kilometer 331 was restricted due to strikes on road infrastructure, with a detour route organized. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178)
In Kharkov, a man attacked TCC employees with a knife, killing one military commissar and wounding another; the attacker is wanted. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31180)
Russian forces deployed tactical aviation with aviation means of destruction threatened for frontline areas in the southeastern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/66480)

An enemy reconnaissance UAV was detected on the border in Chernihiv region near Moshchenky, with several drones remaining in the Borzna, Berezna, and Dmitrivka area of Chernihiv region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98263, @eRadarrua/98218)
Drones were detected near Slavutich in Chernihiv region and near Kraviatychi in Kyiv region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98274)

Russian drones tracked across Sumy region included routes toward Shostka, Mykolaivka, Putyvl, Okhtyrka, and areas near Esman and Novhorod-Siverskyi. (Source: @kpszsu/66469, @eRadarrua/98257, @kpszsu/66499)

Russian forces targeted fuel infrastructure in Sumy region, with 13 documented attacks on gas stations in the current month alone, including three separate strikes on June 24-25 in Zarichnyi district of Sumy city and Kovoakivskyi district. (Source: @eRadarrua/98253)
A ballistic missile was launched toward Kyiv, and Russian forces conducted group strikes on TCC and weapons storage facilities in the capital. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68045, @tass_agency/382553)

SBU struck Russian military support vessels 'Volga' and 'Vyatka' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, targeting submarine reconnaissance systems, mine-laying capabilities, and S-400 components covering the Kerch Strait. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

In Kherson region, a man was killed in Novaya Zburyevka (Holopristansky district) from a UAV attack; a civilian was wounded by UAV strike in Kalanchak; a man was wounded when a UAV struck a truck on the M-17 'Kherson-Dzhankoy' highway in Kalanchak district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178)
In Crimea and Sevastopol, regional emergency regimes were introduced for economic decision-making, according to Aksenov. (Source: @tass_agency/382552)
GUR MO conducted urgent fundraising for long-range FPV drones on fiber optics specifically capable of destroying Russian equipment heading toward Crimea. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68080)
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga stated Belarus is not expected to enter the war, noting Ukraine would mirror any Belarusian involvement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191)

Multiple Shahed drones targeted Kremenchuk HES (at least 3 separate strikes), Kremenchuk TES, and Kremenchuk oil refinery in Poltava region within a concentrated timeframe, with massive attacks on energy infrastructure across Poltava region. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68069, @mon1tor_ua/68068, @mon1tor_ua/68062, @mon1tor_ua/68072, @mon1tor_ua/68071)
Russian forces deployed reactive-type drones ('Geranium-4/5') launched from Orel toward multiple targets across northern and eastern Ukraine. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68044)

A ballistic missile was launched toward Kyiv, and Russian tactical aviation was active in the southeastern direction with threats of aviation means of destruction for frontline areas. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68045, @kpszsu/66480)

Russian drones were tracked across multiple regions including Chernihiv (Moshchenky border area, Borzna, Berezna, Dmitrivka, Slavutich), Sumy (toward Shostka, Mykolaivka, Putyvl, Okhtyrka), Kharkiv (toward Bohodukhiv, Balakliya, Vovchany, Izyum), and Kyiv region (near Kraviatychi). (Source: @eRadarrua/98263, @eRadarrua/98218, @eRadarrua/98274, @kpszsu/66469, @eRadarrua/98257, @kpszsu/66499, @kpszsu/66496)
In Zaporizhzhia region, a Ukrainian UAV struck a private household causing a residential fire; a drone attacked a food delivery vehicle in Kamenka-Dneprovskaya. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178)
In Kherson region, UAV attacks killed one person in Novaya Zburyevka and wounded civilians in Kalanchak and on the M-17 highway. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178)

key_developments: SBU struck Russian military vessels 'Volga,' 'Vyatka,' and ferry 'Petropavlovsk' in occupied Crimea, along with S-400 components covering the Kerch Strait, targeting submarine reconnaissance and mine-laying capabilities. (Source: @UkraineNow/82191)

Massive Russian drone campaign targeted Kremenchuk HES, TES, and oil refinery with multiple Shahed strikes within hours, alongside broader attacks on Poltava region energy infrastructure. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68069, @mon1tor_ua/68062, @mon1tor_ua/68071)
Russia and Ukraine exchanged 160 prisoners of war each, confirmed by the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31187)
Russian ballistic missile struck Zaporizhzhia injuring at least six people, and group strikes targeted TCC and weapons storage facilities in Kyiv. (Source: @UkraineNow/82190, @tass_agency/382553)
President Zelensky signed decree mandating at least 26% of GDP for security and defense in 2027 budget, with NSDC Secretary Umerov overseeing execution. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23597)

NATO Secretary General Rutte stated Russian oil refinery production decreased by one-third and praised Ukrainian drone innovations successfully striking Russian energy and defense infrastructure. (Source: @UkraineNow/82188)

At the Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdansk, 160 agreements worth over €10 billion were signed including €3.2 billion first EU tranche and $3.4 billion World Bank funding. (Source: @UkraineNow/82185)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is entering a phase of intensified strategic strikes on both sides. Ukraine's successful targeting of Russian military vessels in Crimea and S-400 components represents a significant degradation of Russian defensive capabilities in the Kerch Strait area. Russia's concentrated campaign against Ukrainian energy infrastructure — particularly the repeated strikes on Kremenchuk HES, TES, and oil refinery — indicates a deliberate strategy to cripple Ukraine's energy grid ahead of summer heat waves, with temperatures of 35-38°C forecast for June 28-30. The deployment of Geranium-4/5 drones from Orel and continued ballistic missile launches toward Kyiv demonstrate Russia's ability to sustain long-range strike operations despite claimed production decreases.
sections.regional_response: European countries placed health services on heightened alert due to record heat waves, with France confirming at least 55 heat-related deaths and Paris reaching 40.9°C. The European Commission proposed revoking EU asylum for newly arrived Ukrainians of mobilization age and launched a 'voluntary return' program for Ukrainian refugees. Hungary refused to sign an EU proposal to open negotiations with Ukraine on additional issues, with PM Magyar calling it illogical. At the Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdansk, 160 agreements worth over €10 billion were signed, including the first EU tranche of €3.2 billion and World Bank financing of $3.4 billion. NATO Secretary General Rutte publicly acknowledged Ukraine's strong performance and recommended increasing support to Kyiv.
sections.intelligence_notes: The FSB claimed that SBU agent Kyrylo Makarenko coordinated terrorist activities including a teenager detained in Dagestan who allegedly called for organizing a terrorist attack in the US in retaliation for the detention of recruited radicals abroad. Russian authorities introduced regional emergency regimes in Crimea and Sevastopol for economic decision-making purposes. Former Russian Defense Minister Sergei Ivanov died at age 73. The Russian Justice Ministry expanded its foreign agents registry to include 'Crimean Solidarity,' 'Echo' project, sociologist Elena Koneva, and 'Autonomous Action' media group. Russian Deputy Degtyarev stated Russia would seek to strip Romania of the right to conduct competitions. Budanov called the recent Ukraine-Poland conflict a 'terrible mistake' and warned that escalation with neighbors always causes serious political and economic problems.
